chunks.text: HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Criminal Bail Application No.S-364 of 2025 <><><><> Applicant: Azad Khan s/o Dodo @ Daru Khan Through Mr. Zafar Hayat, Advocate. Respondent: Complainant: The State Through Mr. Neel Parkash, D.P.G. Muhammad Ashraf s/o Allah Juriro (Present in Person). Date of Hearing: 09.02.2026 Date of Order: 09.02.2026 <><><><> O R D E R Miran Muhammad Shah, J-: Applicant Azad Khan seeks post-arrest bail in Crime No.272 of 2025 for offence under Sections 320, 279, 337-G, 427, 322, 114, 334, 337-A(i), 337-F(i), 337-A(iv), 337-L(ii) P.P.C registered at Police Station Shahdadpur, after dismissal of his bail plea by the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Shahdadpur, vide order dated 17.11.2025. 2. The details and particulars of the F.I.R are already available in bail application and the F.I.R, as such, need not to reproduce the same hereunder. 3. The complainant is present in person and has filed affidavit stating therein that compromise has been taken place between the parties outside the Court and he has no objection if the bail be granted to the applicant/accused. 4. Learned counsel for the applicant states that the compromise application shall be filed before the learned trial Court for disposal of the matter and acquittal of the applicant/accused on the ground of compromise. 5. The learned D.P.G, looking to the above position, has recorded his no objection. 2 6. In the light of affidavit filed by the complainant, the applicant/accused is admitted to post-arrest bail subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) and a P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court. 7.

The learned D.P.G, looking to the above position, has recorded his no objection. 2 6. In the light of affidavit filed by the complainant, the applicant/accused is admitted to post-arrest bail subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) and a P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court. 7. The observations made here-in-above are tentative in nature and would not prejudice the case of either party at the trial. The application stands disposed of. JUDGE *Adnan Ashraf Nizamani*
